_meta.source: Nerdy official competitive positioning + Meta Ad Library patterns

varsity_tutors_position.core_offer: Monthly 1-on-1 SAT tutoring membership — not a course, not a group class, not a self-study app
varsity_tutors_position.defensible_white_space: 1-on-1 personalization + top 5% vetted tutors + 3.4M learner ratings + 24-hour matching
key_differentiators: Matched with a tutor in 24 hours based on exact weak areas — not random assignment
Top 5% vetted tutors — scored high on their own SAT, attended top 100 university, passed rigorous background and teaching checks
100 points per month at 2 sessions per week plus 20 minutes daily practice
2.6x the score improvement of group classes, local tutors, and learning centers
Digital SAT interface training — the SAT is taken on a laptop, we train on a laptop
Built-in tools training — over 60% of test takers run out of time; our tutors teach the built-in calculator and formulas that can solve questions in 15 seconds instead of 75
Weekly progress reports — parents see score trajectory every week not just at the end
Perfect tutor match guarantee — rematched immediately if not the right fit
varsity_tutors_position.positioning_rule: Every ad must lean into personal matching outcomes. Never use generic 'we have tutors' copy.

competitors.name: Princeton Review
competitors.weakness: Group class pace — your child moves at the class's speed not their own
competitors.our_counter: For what Princeton Review charges for group classes we deliver 1-on-1. Their 1-on-1 is $199-$252/hr extra on top.
competitors.hook_to_steal: Specific score numbers in headline, test date urgency
competitors.what_to_avoid: Do not copy their guarantee framing — we do not offer score guarantees

competitors.name: Khan Academy
competitors.weakness: No accountability, no adaptation, no one explaining what the student gets wrong
competitors.our_counter: Khan Academy raised her score 10 points. That was the problem. Free SAT tutoring is great — until you get your score back.
competitors.what_to_avoid: Do not compete on price — we compete on results

competitors.name: Kaplan
competitors.weakness: Institutional and corporate tone, group format
competitors.our_counter: Same price range as Kaplan group classes — we do 1-on-1
competitors.what_to_avoid: Do not mimic formal institutional tone

competitors.name: Local tutors and learning centers (Sylvan, Kumon, Mathnasium)
competitors.price: $80-$200/hr with no platform, no plan, no practice tests
competitors.weakness: No diagnostic, no plan built for the child, no progress tracking, in-person only
competitors.our_counter: Local tutors charge $80-$200/hr with no platform and no plan. We include diagnostics, a study plan, practice tests, and weekly progress reports.
competitors.hook_to_steal: In-person credibility — flip it: the SAT is digital, train digital
competitors.what_to_avoid: Do not attack in-person tutoring emotionally — flip it logically

competitors.name: Self-study (apps, books, prep books)
competitors.weakness: No accountability, no one identifying what the student gets wrong, score barely moves
competitors.our_counter: 10x the score improvement of self-study. Watching videos on Khan isn't the same as learning to test well on the SAT.
competitors.hook_to_steal: Frustration validation — 'if you've been studying but the score hasn't moved'

meta_ad_patterns_that_work: Specific score gaps in headline: '3.8 GPA. 1260 SAT. Something's off.'
Real calendar urgency: 'Your SAT is in 6 weeks. Here's what's still possible.'
Mechanism specificity: '100 points per month at 2 sessions/week' beats '200+ points'
Conditional claims: 'If your child is between 1100-1300 they can gain 200 points in 8 weeks'
Competitor reframe: 'For what Princeton Review charges for group classes we deliver 1-on-1'
Digital SAT angle: 'The SAT is digital now. Why prep on paper?'
Built-in tools angle: 'Over 60% of test takers run out of time. Our tutors fix that.'
Hook then 2-3 line body then micro-commitment CTA — tight structure wins

question: 3.8 GPA. 1260 SAT. Something's off.
If she's capable of a 1450, why is she stuck in the 1200s?
Smart kids underestimate the SAT. Is yours prepared?
Is your child's SAT score keeping up with their GPA?
stat: 100 points per month. 2 sessions per week. That's the math.
10x the score improvement of Khan Academy. Same time. Same effort.
Over 60% of SAT test takers run out of time. Ours don't.
story: He scored 1180 twice. Then we found the 3 question types he kept missing.
She had a 3.9 GPA and a 1240 SAT. Eight weeks later: 1430.
They tried Khan Academy. The score went up 10 points. Then they called us.
fear: Your SAT is in 6 weeks. Here's what's still possible.
Same score twice? That's fixable — if you start now.
Not the score you expected? Here's why it happened.
empathy: Khan Academy raised her score 10 points. That was the problem.
You've tried everything. The score barely moved. We hear you.
Tutoring didn't work last time? Here's what we do differently.
